title: Markup conformance

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Markup conformance; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Markup conformance
sources.claim: Conformance validation finds markup that violates HTML parsing or content-model requirements before browser recovery hides defects.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Conformance validation finds markup that violates HTML parsing or content-model requirements before browser recovery hides defects.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A validator identifies a button nested inside a link, and the structure is redesigned instead of relying on browser repair. Your goal is to resolve all material conformance errors in a multi-page document set. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Metadata and accessibility audit; Images and media; Data tables; Forms and native controls; Document tree and DOM concept; Native constraints and error semantics. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Conformance validation finds markup that violates HTML parsing or content-model requirements before browser recovery hides defects. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: A conforming document can still be inaccessible or misleading, and embedded templates or frameworks can require validating rendered output too. This draft therefore rejects: If a page looks correct in one browser, HTML conformance errors cannot matter.

includes: Use validator findings as evidence while distinguishing errors from style preferences.
Resolve all material conformance errors in a multi-page document set.
excludes: A conforming document can still be inaccessible or misleading, and embedded templates or frameworks can require validating rendered output too.

outcomes: Resolve all material conformance errors in a multi-page document set.

examples.title: Markup conformance: concrete case
examples.situation: A validator identifies a button nested inside a link, and the structure is redesigned instead of relying on browser repair.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A validator identifies a button nested inside a link, and the structure is redesigned instead of relying on browser repair.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Validate authored and rendered HTML, fix structural errors, and still perform accessibility and content review afterward.
examples.result: The result follows because Validate authored and rendered HTML, fix structural errors, and still perform accessibility and content review afterward.

examples.title: Markup conformance: boundary case
examples.situation: A conforming document can still be inaccessible or misleading, and embedded templates or frameworks can require validating rendered output too.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: A conforming document can still be inaccessible or misleading, and embedded templates or frameworks can require validating rendered output too.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: If a page looks correct in one browser, HTML conformance errors cannot matter.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Validate authored and rendered HTML, fix structural errors, and still perform accessibility and content review afterward.

limitations: Documents cannot model parser recovery, browser APIs, or interactive semantics exactly.
